title: On Matsushiro
wikitext:
[]: On Matsushiro             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Oct 15, 2007 4:40 pm Well, I just found out that in Matsuhiro, there is the remains of the half completed underground network of the Daihonei (Imperial General Headquarters). I wonder if there is any connection.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Oct 15, 2007 4:55 pm What are you talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 15, 2007 4:59 pm The facility where EVA-03 is activated may be the re-furbished redoubt of the Imperial Japanese forces. That sounds plausible to me.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Oct 15, 2007 5:07 pm Wait, you mean those places were NOT torn down after the disestablishment of Imperial Japanese military? :?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Oct 15, 2007 5:09 pm No, it is a museum now.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Oct 15, 2007 5:13 pm Oh, I see.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Oct 15, 2007 7:46 pm That's hardly the only thing of interest in Matsushiro; it's the historic section of Nagano, which in NGE is called Tokyo-2 and serves as the capitol of Japan. If anything, the Nerv facility was probably built there because of the relative lack of existing skyscrapers.

[]:             Quote Post by S.A.L » Wed Oct 17, 2007 6:30 am Also remember that Nagano is relatively close to Tokyo-1, and is accessible by Shinkansen, the Nagano (Hokuriku Shinkansen). It's also very secluded, so it's probably a good idea, anyway. Matsushiro was also the former castle town of the Sanada clan.
